I saw Tonya, once a week, for 3 1/2 years. In this time she helped me in several different ways. First of all, Tonya helped give me a new perspective on life and myself. At the time I didn't not know myself very well. Tonya led me to myself, and started me on the path of understanding how to master myself. Before Tonya, outside influences ruled my life. These outside influences had complete control over me and my emotions. Outside influences caused me to feel something and I would act on those feelings. One of the greatest lessons she taught me is that feelings are NOT facts. At the time I was a binge drinker. I was getting drunk once or twice per weekend and usually at least once throughout the work week. I knew this was not a healthy practice and Tonya helped me to be honest with myself about this habit. I was an alcoholic, I just didn't want to believe it and definitely I did not want to quit drinking. Tonya never once came out and told me that I was an alcoholic, she just helped open my eyes to it. Tonya is a true professional and a master at her craft. Tonya helped to guide me through some extremely dark times. She was there every step of the way. I want to be very clear, Tonya did not do any of these things for me. She gave me the tools,education, and resources to tackle these things myself. Therapy is definitely a two way street. Tonya gave me the means to get better, but it was up to me to put these resources into practice, to help myself.   During this time I had a successful career in the construction industry, but it came at a price. I was a people pleaser, and an alcoholic struggling with massive anxiety and depression which I tried to hide from the outside world. I would let people walk all over me and never stood up for myself in fear of making people mad at me. I wanted to be accepted by the wrong people, so bad, that I would do anything to earn their acceptance. At the time I thought all this was normal. I was a mess when I came to see Tonya! She helped me to sort this mess out and allowed me to become a person I was proud of and wanted to take care of. I now know that it is ok to be true to myself and express my feelings and opinions. I have found greater success in life and my career by doing these things than I ever did as a people pleaser who fell in line and didn't complain. I turned my focus inward on taking care of myself instead sacrificing myself to try and please or take care of others around me. As a result I am a much happier person and I am proud of the person I have become. I haven't drank since 2018. Removing my drinking really opened the door for me to a much healthier and happier life that I am very proud of. And as an added bonus, I no longer have to go through brutal hangovers! I can honestly say, I do not miss it at all. I am also much more confident and comfortable in social situations where I used to rely on drinking as a social lubricant to aid in my social life.
I could go on and on, but I think you get the picture at this point. Tonya is one of the 2 greatest therapists I have ever seen! Going to see Tonya will be one of the best investments you can make in yourself. Just remember, when you go into therapy, try to keep an open mind and be ready to do the work on yourself. It's not always easy but totally worth it! Tonya is a true life saver! You won't regret it!

Dear Jalesa,

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are truly sorry to hear about your experience and regret that you feel our services did not meet your expectations. At Schum Law, we take pride in providing respectful, professional, and diligent legal representation to every client, and we deeply regret if you feel that we fell short in your case.

To address a few of the concerns you raised:
- We strive to guide and assist clients through what can understandably be a stressful process. Communication is key, and we regret if our approach or explanations were unclear or came across as anything but helpful.
- Our intent in following up with you after your initial request to terminate was to ensure we could clarify any misunderstandings or concerns and continue advocating for you if needed.
- Regarding the settlement and fees, we adhere strictly to the terms of our contract and to all legal and ethical guidelines. If there were any misunderstandings about the settlement or costs, we are happy to review these with you in detail.

We are also deeply committed to providing equitable service to all clients, and we take concerns about professionalism very seriously. The claim regarding our treatment of POC is one we do not take lightly. Discrimination has no place in our practice, and we are constantly striving to ensure that every client is treated with respect and dignity.

We would like the opportunity to address your concerns directly. Please reach out to us so we can discuss your experience and any outstanding issues. Our goal is always to provide the best possible outcome and ensure that every client feels heard and supported.

Thank you for giving us the opportunity to clarify and learn from this feedback. We wish you and your family all the best moving forward.

Sincerely,
Todd Schum
Schum Law, P.C.

In 2018, I was stuck in the middle of a drawn-out custody battle that took up my time, money, and patience. Fed up, I started looking for a new law firm to represent me and my son. From day one, Cari took the lead. She listened empathetically, understood the struggles we were going through, and planned a strategic way forward. Attentive to our needs, she made this an efficient process.

Every step of the way, she was candid about the situation. I appreciated her straightforward, no-nonsense approach, even if it wasn't what I wanted or expected to hear. Despite her busy schedule and other client obligations, she always advocated for us, making our case the priority. After months of drawn-out back and forth, when our case finally went to court, our permanent parenting plan was established and gave me more custody than I had expected.

Looking back, Cari was the hope I needed during a dark time. As a woman, she understood the struggles of being a single mom, listened non-judgmentally, and treated me with the utmost respect. Despite all the motions that were tied up in the case, she remained focused, revealing the truth without attacking. She made something emotionally draining, dignified. To this day, she has checked in on us, offered her advice, and continues to be a presence in our lives. I am forever grateful and would recommend Rincker Law to anyone seeking justice for their family.

reviews.text: Cheri helped me through a difficult divorce with kids involved. She gave me the information I needed for each and every step in detail. She was incredibly professional, keeping us informed every step of the way, and managed to get the process moving extremely quickly.
​Beyond her expertise, Cherie is remarkably personable and has a warm, motherly presence that immediately put us at ease during a stressful time. She is genuinely sweet and kind, making us feel like more than just a case file. Our family has officially switched all of our legal matters over to Cherie, and we couldn’t be happier to have her in our corner going forward!"

Cherie Kesler was incredibly helpful and supportive during a difficult time. I highly recommend!

Mrs. Kesler, has been nothing short of professionally outstanding! The ETHICS, commitment, straight to the point,  not one sided, keeps us informed, gets back with you in a timely manner says so much about her personally and professionally. She fights for her clients and wants the best. Oh and she’s on standby if I need her Thank you!

I was very fortunate to have found Cherie Kesler to be my divorce attorney.
My situation was unusual as my husband was the one who filed for divorce in Illinois which is where he resides.  I live in another state which required me to find someone in Illinois to represent me.  I was impressed with my first phone call to her as she was the one who answered the phone and took the time right then and there to talk to me.  Throughout the whole process she answered emails and phone calls promptly and always kept me updated.  She was extremely thorough and efficient and her knowledge and expertise resulted in a good and fast resolution.  I couldn’t have been happier with the outcome and I am happy to highly recommend her!

Cherie was amazing in both of my divorces. She always kept me up to date and she made sure to explain everything in detail that I could understand.   Both divorces were done in no more than 3 months  each time.  I would highly recommend her as an attorney she truly advocates for her clients!

Responsive and resolution focused. Her pragmatic approach and years of experience working with brokenness will save you time and money if you heed her advice. Some won't appreciate her wisdom and will be blinded by greed and anger. She will never waste your time or your money!

I had the displeasure of witnessing Ms. Kesler’s work today. The first concerning act was that while in the courtroom, she greeted opposing counsel by having small talk and catching up on recent life events. It felt inappropriate given the setting and circumstance. She went on to share during her opening about how she does not feel well, centering herself as the focus, which also felt inappropriate and irrelevant. She then accepted a deal in the judge’s chambers without consulting her client about the terms and appeared defensive when confronted with this and her client’s displeasure over it. Shortly after the hearing and discussion, she suggested that the relationship between herself and her client be terminated. Ms. Kesler did not appear to be empathetic, she lacked professionalism, and she did not seem competent. She gave the impression of being disinterested, failed to make any effort to advocate for her client when given the opportunity, and she did not appear prepared for the hearing. I left concerned and disappointed.
From what I witnessed, I would not recommend her services to anyone I care about, especially not for family law.

Here is what I can say about Cherie's legal help. As I am still going through the settlement part of a divorce, Cherie has worked hard to make sense for me what the law says and what it's limitations and risks are. Divorce has been emotional and left me exhausted in EVERY way. Cherie has been the "tough love" voice of reason that has made it make sense. If you need a therapist go find one. If you need someone to coddle your bruised ego and lack of willingness to have the reality of what the law can do, call your mom. But, if you need someone who understands the the rules, risks, applications, and stark realities of what the law dictates, hire Cherie Kessler! She is efficient, practical, and will not mince yours' or her own time with matters that are not relevant to your case. Cherie is a "get things done" attorney that refreshingly brings 3rd and 4th  solutions to the table when matters get stuck! Money well spent!!

Cherie took over my divorce case that had been languishing and got it through quickly and efficiently. She also had good counsel as we moved forward when emotions were running high and things were difficult to navigate.

Amazing to work with! Made the process super quick and easy!
Explained everything as it needed to be.
10/10 recommend!!!

I have had the great pleasure of consulting with Attorney Cherie Kesler on an estate matter for an elderly relative. She clearly has long-time legal knowledge and experience regarding estate law and related matters. But what has impressed me the most is her compassionate, thoughtful attention. She has taken time to carefully explain everything I had questions about and has done so in a way that is ideal for the layman. I appreciate how well she answers questions with both kind patience and legal acumen. And, her prompt response time is a rare quality these days. What a gem!

I watched Cherie Kelser in court the other day treat her client with the utmost disrespect. Quit on her client before the trial even started then when her client was out of the courtroom—talked to the opposing attorney about her client saying all these horrible things. She is not the attorney she advertises to be. I would never recommend her to anyone. Read her other reviews one even comments that all her good reviews are her friends just to boost her ratings.

What first caught my attention about Spiro’s Law was their radio commercial. They spoke directly to the fears and uncertainty that come with trying to find an attorney and navigating such a daunting process. It truly felt like they understood exactly what people in our position were going through. After hearing the commercial and reading their online reviews, I knew they were the firm I wanted to trust with our serious work accident case.

From the very beginning, they made the entire process feel seamless and manageable. They took the time to answer all of our questions, kept us informed, and worked hard to keep everything moving along as efficiently as possible. Cases like ours involve a lot of information and patience, but their team guided us through every step with professionalism and care.

I especially want to mention Miranda. She is the perfect balance of kind, compassionate, and incredibly strong when it comes to advocating for her clients. She has such a warm personality, but when it’s time to fight for you, she is absolutely fierce and relentless in the best way possible. It’s easy to see why she has the reputation she does. She truly stands up for people who have been injured and makes sure their voices are heard.

We went into this knowing absolutely nothing about how attorneys or legal cases work, and there are so many mixed opinions about who you should hire for something this important. I can honestly say we could not be happier with the outcome or the experience we had with their team.

Our family is incredibly grateful for the way they advocated for us and fought for the best possible result. Most importantly, they earned our trust during a very difficult time.

Thank you, Spiro’s Law, for everything you did for our family!

If you’re looking for a trustworthy and dependable law office, look no further!

reviews.text: I was on the losing end of an auto vs. pedestrian impact, with all the devastation you can imagine comes with that. My family coordinated an appointment with Spiros because I was a little out of it when I first came home from the hospital. Within minutes of the first meeting I knew I was in good hands. They were empathetic to the tragedy I had survived, understood how difficult recovery was going to be for me, plus the fact that it would be close to 6 months until I could start back to work, and that it would be a slow return to work back up to full-time. They made sure I received everything I could be compensated for and even took on my health insurance who was demanding an outrageous lien. Spiros fought to make sure I would come through all right and I did, thanks to their dedicated, professional services. I highly recommend!!

reviews.text: Brian King of over promising and underdelivering, hire someone else
reviews.ownerResponse:
I took some time before responding just to make sure I would respond as objectively as possible. And, as I told Leigh Anne shortly after the review was posted, I acknowledge that there was a period of time during the case when I was not as responsive as I should have been. However, and I think she would have to agree, the biggest reason her case was delayed was due to her ex filing for bankruptcy while the divorce case was pending. The end result was one that Leigh Anne agreed to after a long morning of back and forth negotiations with opposing counsel and the Judge, so I really do disagree with the characterization that I over-promised and under-delivered. In the conversation we had after the review, which was long after the case was over, I asked her what she meant by that. She said that she feels like I promised her it would take much less time and cost far less in fees to resolve her case than it ended up taking/costing. I can say with 100% certainty that did not PROMISE a specific timeline or a specific fee range. I am sure that I probably said that, based on what she told me at the initial consult, I think it should take ___ months and cost approximately ___ dollars if that is how the case goes. But I am also SURE that I would follow that up with a clarification that it is impossible to say for sure how long any given case will take (and, accordingly, how much it will cost). I can say this with 100% certainty because I am very careful to NOT do those things in ANY case.

Having said all of that, I absolutely harbor no ill will toward Leigh Anne and wish her the best moving forward into this next stage of her life. And I regret the perception on her part, though I believe that the blame is more appropriately attributable to her ex husband, his bankruptcy filing and his general unreasonableness when it came to trying to settle without having to go to court.
Thanks!

I sold my home in December 2025, and Jenny Park served as my real estate attorney. She was recommended by my agent, and I accepted the referral without interviewing her first. If I had spoken with her beforehand, I would not have chosen to work with her.
To be fair, she did keep the timeline on track. However, her attitude and professionalism were extremely disappointing.
1. Rudeness during closing
When I asked questions about the documents I was signing, the first response I received was a loud, frustrated sigh. My wife witnessed it as well. Moments later, she raised her voice at me as if I couldn’t hear or understand her. My hearing is perfectly fine. As a client, I expect clear explanations of the documents I’m signing—especially from the attorney I hired. Instead, I felt dismissed and talked down to.
2. Careless mistakes in my documents
I found multiple errors in my personal information across different documents, including a phone number I’ve never had and an address where I’ve never lived. These mistakes seriously damaged my trust in the accuracy of her work.
3. Lack of transparency about fees
Be prepared for unexpected legal fees. She never explained or disclosed any of the costs ahead of time. I ended up paying over $2,000 in attorney fees at closing. If you have questions, you’ll need to research them yourself because you won’t get proactive guidance from her.
Overall
She completes the work on time, but her rudeness, lack of communication, and careless errors in official documents make her very difficult to recommend. I would not work with her again.
Not only do they do a magnificent job with my LLC filings and refilling, but they do it with a very human touch. I highly recommend choosing Meyer Capel for all of your legal needs.

This is in response to the review I made 4 weeks ago. Meyer Capel asked me to reach out to them upon my poor review. I did; it took them 1 week to reply and gave me very little notice and very little options of when I could speak with them. Stephanie and Steven spoke with me on my 3/19 call. They said they would bring my concerns up to the board meeting and I would hear back the following week. I didn't hear back upon their promised date. I had to email asking for an update.

It just goes to confirm my previous experience of being ignored, files lost, Docusigns Lost, information printed incorrectly on my Divorce Certificate, not advocating for their client.

I would give a zero or negative stars if I could. If you need a divorce, this is not the law from you should hire unless you want the process drawn out, a lawyer not to fight for you, documents to get lost, and to experience a high turnover in lawyers and paralegals. When I first met with Tristan (Lawyer #1) he advised me I would have to wait a little over a month before he could start handling my case. I was ok with that. I signed paperwork, paid my retainer and waited 2 months. I sent an email following up and was told my case was actually marked as "Closed". They re-opened it and I had to wait a couple more weeks before he could fit me in. I was advised that we could pass up my Financial Affidavit because they thought an agreement could be made. It couldn't but I was not advised otherwise until months later. I compiled my financial affidavit and sent it off. Waited and asked for an update. I believe I sent 3 emails over the course of 1 month to Tristan and Helen, his paralegal. I never heard anything from them. I had to contact my Ex- to ask his lawyer if they got my financial affidavit. It was never sent, and in fact Tristan hadn't even looked at it. To Tristan's credit, he did apologize and refund me some retainer money. He told me to expect a response within 24 business hours. Tristan wound up moving to a different state and passed my case along to a different lawyer in the office, Jilmala (Lawyer #2). Jilmala would take weeks to reply to me. I routine had to follow up on action items and then follow up on my follow up. During all of this I went through like 9 different paralegals and interns - Helen, Kelsi, Sandra, Shannon, Heather, Ashley, Caroline, Samantha, and Stephanie. I would sign documents in DocuSign with one of them and it would be lost. Id then be contacted weeks later asking me to sign a document I'd already sign but they had lost. The marital house was sold in the midst of the divorce, we had legal paperwork on how the funds would be handled. Month later when Jilmala was typing up the final MSA, she included how the house would be listed, sold, and funds dispersed. I had to tell her and send her documents of my house closing that they should have already had on file. It was an extremely frustrating experience and it escalated what could have been a more friendly divorce into a nit-picking and feisty one because of how long it dragged out. I did not feel like I had any advocates on my side. I was not counseled in any direction just asked what offer I wanted to make? or if an offer from the other lawyer was one I would want to accept. They basically were just an expensive vehicle to file all the legal paperwork for my divorce. No lawyers fought for me. Photos are of my emails with the various lawyers and paralegals; please note dates on the email threads. I would identify them as a Circus Ring and not a professional Law Firm. The only good thing that came out of this was I did eventually get a Cert of Marriage Dissolution; however that was originally sent with incorrect information. They had my Ex's state of birth and current county incorrect.
